question_text: A car engine converts chemical energy from petrol into mechanical energy to move the car. If the engine has an efficiency of 25%, what happens to the other 75% of the energy?
correct_answer: The other 75% of energy is wasted, mainly as heat through the exhaust gases and engine cooling systems. Some is also lost due to friction in moving parts.
explanation: Car engines are relatively inefficient; most of the energy from burning fuel is wasted as heat. This is why cars need cooling systems and why exhaust gases are hot. This real-world example helps students understand why energy efficiency matters.
